_publications._publicationTitle: Transient inhibition of the ERK pathway prevents cerebellar developmental defects and improves long-term motor functions in murine models of neurofibromatosis type 1.

_contextSnippet: the improved motor performance was maintained at least 1 month after MEKi treatment was terminated, though the rescue in motor learning was lost
details: Improved motor performance was maintained at least 1 month after MEKi treatment was terminated, though rescue in motor learning was lost
